cxu_object.claim: After 80 hours of tracking signal conviction against price movements, options-skew is the only signal with consistent directional accuracy at 55-56 percent while regime signal is anti-useful at 38 percent and should be ignored or inverted and most other signals cluster around 48-52 percent which is noise

supporting_contexts.text: Conviction tracker analysis over 4,812 data points at 5-minute resolution. Per-signal accuracy: options-skew 56% bull / 55% bear (best directional), bb-reversal 52%, mesa 52% (high conf but one-directional), regime 38% (worst, high-conf only 29%), oi 42%, cross-asset 46%. Signals with >50% overall: bb-reversal, consensus, mesa, funding, trend-exhaustion. Signals below 50%: basis, onchain-flow, options-skew overall but strong directional, technical, momentum, cross-asset, oi, regime.

Key insight: stronger conviction does NOT improve accuracy for most signals. NEUTRAL predictions (no direction) are 63% accurate — the most reliable signal is when signals collectively say nothing will happen. For directional trades, weight options-skew heavily and ignore regime entirely.
